channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: One Nation's popularity surge shows signs of slowing amid shifting political dynamics
item.description: Recent opinion polls indicate that One Nation's surge in popularity, which followed the Bondi terror attack in December 2025, is beginning to wane. While the party's primary vote dropped to 26% in the...

item.title: South Korean Ex-President Sentenced to Two Years for Political Funding Violations
item.description: A South Korean court has sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to two years in prison after finding him guilty of illegally receiving opinion polling services worth 270 million won ($179,800) from ...

item.title: Plea Deal Collapse in SAPS Tender Corruption Case
item.description: The plea deal between the Investigative Directorate Against Corruption (Idac) and alleged criminal cartel member Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala has collapsed. Matlala had previously agreed to an eight-year di...

item.title: Joaquin Tovera Transfers to La Salle After Leaving UP
item.description: Joaquin Tovera, a prominent basketball player, has officially committed to joining La Salle University after his departure from the University of the Philippines (UP). This decision marks a significan...

item.title: Rising Cases of Anaplasmosis Highlight Growing Public Health Concern in Canada
item.description: A new case report published in the Canadian Medical Association Journal (CMAJ) warns of the rapid rise of anaplasmosis, a tick-borne bacterial illness, across Canada. The report highlights how the dis...

item.title: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Condemns Death of Former Qatari Emir Sheikh Hamad
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif visited Doha to express condolences for the death of former Qatari Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who passed away at 74. The visit, accompanied by Nawaz Sharif a...

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel mocks Mitch McConnell's proof-of-life photo after weeks of speculation
item.description: Late-night host Jimmy Kimmel returned from summer vacation to mock Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell's long-awaited proof-of-life photo, which was released nearly a month after the 84-year-old Ke...

item.title: Clavicular's Earnings Breakdown: Sources of Income and Net Worth
item.description: Clavicular, an American online streamer and influencer, has built a net worth of approximately $300,000 through multiple income streams. His primary earnings come from live streaming on Kick, where he...

item.title: Jay-Z's Yankee Stadium Concert Faces Security Challenges and Delays
item.description: Jay-Z's third night at Yankee Stadium was marred by a near four-hour delay caused by fans breaching security gates, prompting a full stadium lockdown. According to reports, a large number of attendees...

item.title: Indigenous History Celebrated Through Artful Rock Formation in Regina
item.description: A striking geological artwork known as the 'New Buffalo' rock formation has been unveiled in Regina, Saskatchewan, as a tribute to Indigenous heritage and history. Created by local artist Mick Favel, ...

item.title: Multi-country African news updates on security operations, migration policies, diplomatic relations and corruption cases
item.description: Nigeria's military successfully rescued 46 children and teachers abducted by militants in Oyo State two months ago, marking a significant counter-terrorism operation. The rescue came after sustained e...

item.title: Villagers Construct Unsafe Bridge to Access Clinic Amid Municipal Delays
item.description: Residents of Mafarafara village near Burgersfort in Limpopo have constructed a makeshift bridge across the Tubatse River to access the Ga-Taung Clinic after years of waiting for the Fetakgomo Tubatse ...

item.title: Nova Scotia donor faces financial strain due to lack of companion support
item.description: Amanda Acker, an Ottawa woman donating her kidney to her sister in Nova Scotia, has revealed the unexpected financial burden of the procedure. While Nova Scotia covers expenses for donors like travel ...

item.title: Two TB Researchers Contract TB Despite Expertise, Highlighting Diagnosis and Treatment Challenges in South Africa
item.description: Two tuberculosis researchers in South Africa, Constance Schreuder and Tatenda Bvudzijena, contracted the disease they study, exposing systemic challenges in TB diagnosis and treatment. Schreuder, a se...

item.title: The Multifaceted Role of Modern Goalkeepers in Soccer
item.description: Goalkeepers in soccer have a unique role that extends beyond just stopping shots. Their training involves developing reflexes, decision-making, and game-reading skills. Modern goalkeepers are not only...

item.title: Angat Dam Water Level Slightly Rises, Still Below Critical Threshold
item.description: The National Water Resources Board (NWRB) reported that the water level at Angat Dam rose slightly as of Monday, July 13, 2026, but remains below the critical 160-meter threshold. This increase is att...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Visits Qatar to Condole Ex-Emir Sheikh Hamad's Passing
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif arrived in Qatar on a one-day visit to express condolences for the passing of former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani. The visit, accompanied by PML-N President Naw...

item.title: Yamal Seeks to Match Mbappes’ World Cup Legacy in Semi-Final Clash
item.description: Lamine Yamal, the Spanish teen sensation, is set to face Kylian Mbappe in a crucial World Cup semi-final clash. At just 19 years old, Yamal aims to follow in Mbappe’s footsteps by winning the tourname...

item.title: Plateau Attack Claims Nine Lives, Including a Baby
item.description: In a tragic incident in Plateau State, nine family members, including a baby, were killed in a violent attack. The attack, which occurred in Riyom Local Government Area, has sparked outrage and calls ...

item.title: ICE Faces Scrutiny Over Lack of Evidence in Houston Shooting Incident
item.description: The article examines the controversy surrounding the shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents, highlighting the absence of conclusive evidence and co...
